title: What to know about Ammi visnaga before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Ammi visnaga are treating asthma due to its bronchodilatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils containing sesquiterpenes, particularly visnagin. It has also been traditionally used to alleviate the symptoms of gout by reducing uric acid levels, a property attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, mainly due to its coumarins, make it effective in treating rheumatism and arthritis, reducing pain and inflammation. Its antipyretic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, allow Ammi visnaga to be used in treating fever. Additionally, its expectorant properties, due to its essential oils containing sesquiterpenes, make it effective in treating bronchitis, epilepsy, diarrhea, and cough.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Ammi visnaga are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is particularly effective against Gram-positive bacteria, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes. Its antioxidant properties are due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant, which can neutralize free radicals and protect cells from damage. Ammi visnaga also exhibits anti-inflammatory activity, primarily through the action of sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Moreover, the plant's analgesic and pain relief properties are attributed to the sesquiterpenes and monoterpenes, which can interact with opioid receptors to reduce pain perception. Additionally, Ammi visnaga's antispasmodic, expectorant, and cardioactive properties are also noteworthy, with the sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids contributing to its ability to relax smooth muscles, liquefy mucus, and modulate cardiac function.
identification: Ammi visnaga is a perennial herb belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its umbels of small white flowers and compound leaves divided into three to five leaflets. It is native to the Mediterranean region, North Africa, and parts of the Middle East, growing in dry, sandy areas and along roadsides. The plant can reach heights of up to 1 meter and has a spreading habit, with its stems and leaves covered in fine hairs. Its geographical distribution includes countries such as Egypt, Israel, and Morocco, and it has also been naturalized in other regions. The plant's growth habit and appearance vary depending on the conditions it grows in, with some sources describing it as a robust perennial and others as an annual or biennial.
cultivation: Ammi visnaga is a biennial herb that prefers well-drained, fertile soil with a pH between 6.0 and 8.0, and full sun to partial shade. It grows to a height of 0.5-1.5 meters, producing a bushy habit with an open crown, and requires support for its tall stems. The plant thrives in dry to moderately moist conditions, making it suitable for areas with low rainfall, and can tolerate some drought. To promote healthy growth, it is recommended to sow seeds in early spring or late summer/early fall, and to avoid overwatering, which can lead to root rot. Regular pruning of the plant's stems and leaves can also help to maintain its shape and encourage root development.
history: Ammi visnaga is a plant native to the Mediterranean region, traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. In ancient Greece, Ammi visnaga was employed by the physician Hippocrates to reduce inflammation and ease pain. The plant's essential oil, extracted from its seeds, contains a compound called asarin, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. In traditional Middle Eastern medicine, Ammi visnaga was used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to reduce fever. The plant's use in medicine was also documented by the Roman naturalist Pliny the Elder, who noted its ability to reduce swelling and ease pain.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a group of medicinal constituents in Ammi visnaga with antiseptic, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga contains Flavonoids, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids present in Ammi visnaga are hydroxylated derivatives of benzoic and cinnamic acids with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: A type of bioactive glycoside compound found in Ammi visnaga, characterized by its amphipathic and surfactant properties.

constituents.name: Furanocoumarins
constituents.description: Furanocoumarins are furan ring-containing compounds found in Ammi visnaga, known for their coumarin derivatives, possessing antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and cardiotonic properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Bulbocapnine is an alkaloid that has sedative, antispasmodic and anticholinergic properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga contains glycosides, which are a type of compound characterized by the presence of a sugar molecule attached to a non-carbohydrate moiety.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga terpenoids have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: A type of phenolic compound found in Ammi visnaga, characterized by a two-phenylpropane skeleton, with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Phenylpropanoids
constituents.description: Phenylpropanoids, found in Ammi visnaga, have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga contains triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpenes
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga contains sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and are also known to have spasmolytic and antitussive effects.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga contains Tannins, which are astringent, antimicrobial and antioxidant compounds.

constituents.name: Coumarin glycosides
constituents.description: Coumarin glycosides are antispasmodic and sedative constituents that have been found in Ammi visnaga.

constituents.name: Phytosterols
constituents.description: Phytosterols are plant sterols with beta-sitosterol, stigmasterol, and campesterol being the most common types, having anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids from Ammi visnaga are flavonoids and caffeic acid derivatives with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and cardiotonic properties.

constituents.name: Ligustilide
constituents.description: A sesquiterpene lactone with sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties

constituents.name: Phthalide
constituents.description: Phthalide is a type of organic compound with a coumarin-like structure and a sweet, earthy odor.

constituents.name: Sterols
constituents.description: Ammi visnaga sterols have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic glycosides
constituents.description: Phenolic glycosides are a group of constituents found in Ammi visnaga, characterized by their glycosidic bonds between phenolic compounds and sugar molecules, contributing to their structural and functional properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Ammi visnaga extracts have been used to induce labor in pregnant women.
Ammi visnaga increases blood pressure in people with hypertension
Uterine contractions persisting beyond 1 minute after childbirth
Photosensitive seizures in individuals with photosensitive epilepsy.
Hypokalemia and renal impairment are worsened in patients with heart failure.
Uric acid levels in the blood.
Ammi visnaga interacts with anticoagulant medications such as warfarin, increasing the risk of bleeding.

intro_description: Ammi visnaga, commonly known as tournesol de mer, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions. Its main medicinal uses include alleviating anxiety and insomnia, reducing inflammation and pain, and lowering blood pressure. The herb has several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The therapeutic properties of Ammi visnaga include anxiolytic, sedative, and vasodilatory effects. Its bioactive compounds include coumarins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, and it is often prepared as a tea or infusion for medicinal use.

uses_list.description: The extracts of this plant have been used to treat asthma due to the presence of furanocoumarins, which have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to relieve bronchospasm and improve lung function.

This plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat gout due to their anti-inflammatory and uricosuric properties, which help to reduce uric acid levels in the body and alleviate the symptoms of gout, such as pain and swelling.

The extracts of this plant have been used to treat rheumatism due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints and muscles, and improve mobility and flexibility.

This plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat fever due to their antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature and alleviate the symptoms of fever, such as sweating and chills.

The extracts of this plant have been used to treat arthritis due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints and improve mobility and flexibility.

This plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat epilepsy due to their anticonvulsant properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of seizures and alleviate the symptoms of epilepsy.

The extracts of this plant have been used to treat diarrhea due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the frequency and severity of diarrhea and alleviate the symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.

This plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat bronchitis due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve bronchospasm, reduce inflammation, and promote the clearance of mucus from the lungs.

The extracts of this plant have been used to treat inflammation due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain, inflammation, and swelling in the body, and improve overall health and well-being.

This plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat cough due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve bronchospasm, reduce inflammation, and promote the clearance of mucus from the lungs.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Ammi visnaga is used to treat various conditions, with asthma being one of the primary uses, as the plant's essential oils have been shown to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation in the airways, which is also beneficial for bronchitis sufferers. Additionally, Ammi visnaga has been found to inhibit uric acid production, making it a potential treatment for gout. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also make it useful for treating arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, such as cough and rheumatism. Furthermore, Ammi visnaga has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones and diarrhea. Its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects also make it a potential treatment for migraine, menstrual cramps, epilepsy, eczema, urinary tract infections, pain, tuberculosis, dyspepsia, varicose veins, and hypertension.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Ammi visnaga is used to treat various health conditions. Asthma symptoms are alleviated due to its expectorant properties, while epilepsy is managed with its neuroprotective effects. Cough and bronchitis are treated with its bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory actions, and migraine is eased with its analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties. Fever and inflammation are reduced with its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory actions, and rheumatism is treated with its anti-arthritic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, Ammi visnaga is used to manage hypertension, gout, eczema, migraines, gastroenteritis, diabetes, dysmenorrhea, menorrhagia, kidney stones, digestive issues, and diarrhea with its diverse range of pharmacological properties.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Ammi visnaga is used to treat asthma by relieving respiratory issues and bronchitis symptoms, while also providing relief from cough and bronchitis. Rheumatism and gout are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties, which also help with arthritis and inflammation. Additionally, Ammi visnaga has been used to treat fever and diarrhea, as well as eczema, due to its antiseptic and antipruritic effects. Its antispasmodic properties make it effective in treating migraines and dyspepsia, while its neuroprotective effects are beneficial in cases of epilepsy, hypertension, and hepatitis. Furthermore, Ammi visnaga is used to treat hemorrhoids, urinary tract infections, menstrual disorders, pneumonia, and digestive issues such as nausea and indigestion, in addition to its antiseptic properties which combat infections.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Ammi visnaga is used to treat various health conditions. It is particularly effective in managing respiratory issues such as asthma, bronchitis, and cough, as well as reducing inflammation, which is beneficial for conditions like rheumatism and arthritis. Additionally, its antipyretic properties make it a useful treatment for fever, while its antidiarrheal properties help alleviate diarrhea and dyspepsia. Ammi visnaga is also used to treat gout, epilepsy, migraine, jaundice, and hysteria, as well as conditions like kidney stones, colic, eczema, and skin infections. Furthermore, it has antimicrobial properties that help combat pneumonia, gastritis, and other infections.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Ammi visnaga is used to treat a range of health conditions, including gout, where it helps to reduce uric acid levels and alleviate pain. It is also employed to alleviate symptoms of asthma, such as difficulty breathing, and is used in the treatment of arthritis, particularly rheumatoid arthritis, to reduce inflammation and joint pain. Furthermore, Ammi visnaga is used to treat bronchitis, a respiratory condition characterized by inflammation of the bronchial tubes, as well as epilepsy, a neurological disorder that affects the brain. Additionally, it is used to alleviate symptoms of kidney stones, fever, inflammation, nephritis, urinary tract infections, cough, hypertension, dyspepsia, menorrhagia, cholecystitis, menstrual cramps, migraine, pneumonia, and tuberculosis.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains a compound known as coumarin, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the body.
The coumarin in this plant has anticoagulant properties, preventing the formation of blood clots and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.
This plant has been found to have antifungal properties, making it effective in treating fungal infections such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
The plant's essential oil has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress levels in individuals.
The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of this plant make it effective in treating skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis.
The plant's diuretic properties help to increase urine production, which can help to flush out kidney stones and reduce the risk of future stone formation.
This plant has been found to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, preventing gastrointestinal issues such as constipation, diarrhea, and nausea.
The plant's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it effective in treating respiratory infections such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
The plant's diuretic and vasodilating properties help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
This plant has been found to have antiparasitic properties, making it effective in treating parasitic infections such as tapeworms and hookworms.

benefits_description: Ammi visnaga reduces inflammation due to its sesquiterpene lactone content, which inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. It also prevents blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation and the formation of thrombin, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular events. Additionally, Ammi visnaga treats fungal infections, including those caused by Candida and Aspergillus species, by disrupting the fungal cell membrane and inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol. The plant's essential oil has anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety levels by modulating the activity of GABA receptors in the brain. Ammi visnaga cures skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, by reducing inflammation and preventing the spread of infection, and it also treats kidney stones by increasing urine production and reducing the concentration of minerals in the kidneys.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Ammi visnaga improves blood flow due to its vasodilatory properties, which relax blood vessel walls to increase blood circulation and reduce blood pressure. Strengthens heart muscles through its cardiotonic properties, improving the heart's pumping efficiency. Enhances circulation by stimulating the removal of blood clots and promoting vasodilation. Boosts oxygenation by increasing the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to tissues, lowering cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of very-low-density lipoproteins. Decreases inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, preventing blood clots from forming by inhibiting platelet aggregation and regulating heart rate to maintain a stable cardiac rhythm.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Ammi visnaga relieves bloating by inhibiting the production of gas in the digestive system due to its carminative properties. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, which helps in soothing irritation and calming digestion, allowing the body to heal from gastritis and ulcers. The antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of Ammi visnaga stimulate appetite and prevent constipation by promoting a healthy gut environment and improving absorption of nutrients. Additionally, the plant's anti-diarrheal properties help in curing diarrhea by reducing the frequency and severity of watery stools. Overall, Ammi visnaga's unique combination of medicinal properties makes it an effective treatment for a range of digestive issues.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Ammi visnaga regulates adrenal function by exerting adaptogenic properties, allowing the body to adapt to stress. It supports thyroid balance due to its thyrotropic activity, stimulating the thyroid gland to produce essential hormones. The plant's hormone-regulating properties also stabilize hormone levels, ensuring a delicate balance between different hormones in the body. Ammi visnaga boosts metabolism rates by enhancing mitochondrial activity, increasing energy production and promoting weight loss. Additionally, it reduces insulin resistance, improves pancreatic function, maintains ovarian health, enhances cortisol response, promotes testosterone balance, and regenerates reproductive tissues due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Ammi visnaga reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory property, which helps to alleviate discomfort and pain associated with various skin conditions. The plant heals wounds quickly by promoting blood flow and accelerating the repair process, thanks to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Ammi visnaga soothes skin irritation by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress, thus providing relief from redness and itching. Its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also prevent skin damage by shielding cells from harm and promoting healthy skin cell growth, thereby safeguarding skin barrier integrity. Ammi visnaga fights skin infections by exhibiting antimicrobial activity that inhibits the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, cures skin rashes by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, repairs skin tissues by stimulating collagen synthesis and promoting wound closure, safeguards skin integrity by maintaining a strong barrier function, and accelerates skin healing by enhancing tissue regeneration and reducing scar formation.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Ammi visnaga boosts lymph flow due to its diuretic properties, stimulating the excretion of excess fluids and promoting the elimination of waste products. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce lymph inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby creating a conducive environment for lymphatic health. Ammi visnaga stimulates lymph nodes by exerting a gentle, yet effective, stimulatory effect on the lymphoid tissue, enhancing its ability to filter out pathogens and toxins. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties increase lymph filtration by neutralizing free radicals and inhibiting the growth of microorganisms, respectively, thus preventing damage to the lymphatic system. By supporting lymph function with its immunomodulatory and vasodilatory effects, Ammi visnaga promotes the overall well-being of the lymphatic system, while its relaxant properties activate lymph circulation, and its regenerative properties protect the lymph tissue from damage and promote its regeneration.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Ammi visnaga reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate joint pain and promote recovery. The plant's antispasmodic property relaxes muscles, making it an effective remedy for muscle cramps and spasms. Its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties ease pain and discomfort, while its chondroprotective properties improve mobility by protecting joint cartilage. Additionally, Ammi visnaga's ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines fights arthritis, and its calcium-regulating properties prevent osteoporosis by maintaining bone density. The plant's collagen-stimulating and tissue-healing properties stimulate collagen production, soothe joints, and heal tendons, making it a valuable natural remedy for musculoskeletal health.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Ammi visnaga relaxes anxiety by acting as an anxiolytic and sedative, while also exhibiting a calming effect on the nervous system. The plant reduces stress through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and mitigate oxidative stress. Ammi visnaga regulates nervousness by exerting a sedative effect on the central nervous system, thus modulating neural activity. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritability by reducing inflammation in the brain, thereby promoting a sense of calmness. The plant enhances clarity by stimulating the release of neurotransmitters that improve cognitive function, and promotes relaxation by increasing the levels of GABA, a neurotransmitter that regulates sleep and relaxation patterns, stabilizes mood by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, boosts energy by acting as a stimulant, calms nerves through its sedative effect, and improves focus by modulating attentional processes.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Ammi visnaga regulates menstruation by exerting antispasmodic properties that ease uterine contractions, thereby providing relief from dysmenorrhea. Ammi visnaga reduces ovulation due to its anti-estrogenic properties, which inhibit the production of estrogen, a hormone crucial for ovulation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ammi visnaga treat dysmenorrhea by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterine lining. Ammi visnaga improves fertility by stimulating the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances essential for ovulation and implantation. Ammi visnaga prevents abortions, enhances ovulation, relieves menstrual cramps, supports pregnancy, cures infertility, and reduces miscarriages due to its ability to strengthen the uterine lining and regulate hormonal imbalances.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Ammi visnaga reduces inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in soothing the respiratory system. Eases congestion by acting as a demulcent, forming a protective barrier over the mucous membranes and allowing them to heal. The antispasmodic and expectorant properties of Ammi visnaga relax airways, making breathing easier. Calms coughs by suppressing the cough reflex and reducing the production of mucus, while its antihistaminic properties prevent allergies by blocking the release of histamine. Ammi visnaga soothes respiratory issues, relieves wheezing, combats bronchitis, removes mucus, and stabilizes breathing due to its antimicrobial and mucolytic properties.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Ammi visnaga relieves kidney strain by its diuretic properties, which stimulate urine production and reduce pressure on the kidneys. The plant's antimicrobial properties treat bladder infections, preventing the spread of bacteria and promoting healing. Ammi visnaga also cures urinary tract issues due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, its antioxidant properties alleviate kidney stones by preventing oxidative stress and promoting the breakdown of stone-forming substances. Ammi visnaga's relaxing properties reduce urine retention by promoting smooth muscle relaxation and improving urine flow, while its healing properties cure bladder ulcers and prostate issues, and its preventative properties prevent kidney damage and treat bladder prolapse.

properties_list.description: Ammi visnaga has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of microorganisms such as bacteria, fungi, and yeast, thereby preventing infections.

Studies have found that Ammi visnaga possesses antioxidant properties, which help in neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress, thus protecting cells from damage and promoting overall health.
Ammi visnaga has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions associated with swelling, redness, and pain.
Research has demonstrated that Ammi visnaga possesses analgesic properties, which help in relieving pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Ammi visnaga has been found to exhibit antispasmodic properties, which help in relaxing muscles and alleviating spasms, cramps, and other muscle-related issues.

Studies have shown that Ammi visnaga possesses expectorant properties, which help in promoting the expulsion of mucus and other secretions from the respiratory tract, thus relieving coughs and congestion.

Ammi visnaga has been reported to provide pain relief by combining its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing pain and inflammation, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting overall well-being.

parts_description: The most used parts of Ammi visnaga for medicinal purposes are flowers, seeds, roots, and leaves. The flowers contain volatile oils, including ligustilide and butylphthalide, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and sedative properties. The seeds, rich in saponins and flavonoids, display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, while the roots, which contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The leaves, rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, demonstrate antioxidant and hepatoprotective activities. These bioactive compounds contribute to the medicinal properties of Ammi visnaga, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative effects.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, particularly visnagin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing relief from pain, reducing swelling and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: seeds
parts_list.description: The seeds of this plant are rich in alkaloids, including visnagin and sesamin, which possess sedative, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory activities, promoting relaxation, soothing muscle spasms, and reducing inflammation and pain.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain a complex mixture of sesquiterpenes, including visnagin and sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing protection against infection, reducing inflammation and pain, and promoting wound healing.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant contain a variety of alkaloids, including sesamin and sesamolin, which possess antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities, soothing muscle spasms, reducing inflammation and pain, and protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat heart failure, edema, and high blood pressure due to its diuretic properties and its ability to increase urine production.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga tincture is widely used to treat edema, high blood pressure, varicose veins, thrombophlebitis, chronic venous insufficiency, and osteoarthritis. It's a very common preparation due to its widespread application in various medical conditions. The tincture's diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects are its most common outcomes. Ammi visnaga tincture is typically made from the seeds and roots of the plant, which are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, responsible for its medicinal properties. Making this preparation is moderately difficult due to the need for precise extraction and concentration of the active compounds.
preparation_recipe: Gather dried Ammi visnaga seeds and roots, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the seeds and roots in a clean glass jar, and cover them with a 1:2 ratio of 80-proof vodka or ethanol.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, allowing the mixture to steep for at least 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place, and use it as needed for medicinal purposes.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and bronchitis, and to reduce inflammation and pain.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga salve is a traditional herbal preparation used to treat various conditions including bursitis, gout, rheumatism, insect bites, bruises and sprains. It's a common treatment found in many folk medicine practices around the world. The salve often has a numbing effect, which is its most notable characteristic. Ammi visnaga salve is made from the seeds and roots of the plant, which are rich in sesquiterpene lactones. However, the process of extracting these compounds is quite complex and time-consuming, making the preparation of ammi visnaga salve a labor-intensive task.
preparation_recipe: Harvest the Ammi visnaga seeds and roots under the correct conditions.
Dry the seeds and roots thoroughly to preserve their properties.
Create a decoction by boiling the dried roots in water, then let it steep.
Strain the decoction and mix it with a carrier oil, such as olive oil, in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture gently and stir until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties, then let it cool and solidify into a salve.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat edema, hypertension, and cardiac insufficiency due to its diuretic properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga decoction is a traditional herbal preparation commonly used to treat various health conditions, including hepatic cirrhosis, hepatitis, hypertension, menstrual disorders, epilepsy, diabetes, cardiovascular disease and kidney stones. It's widely used in many cultures due to its established efficacy. The decoction has a diuretic effect, which helps to eliminate excess fluids from the body. The roots and seeds of the Ammi visnaga plant are used to make the decoction, which requires a moderate level of skill and attention to detail to prepare correctly.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Ammi visnaga roots and seeds, clean them thoroughly, and cut the roots into smaller pieces.
Combine the cut roots and seeds in a saucepan with enough water to cover them, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Store the prepared Ammi visnaga herbal decoction in a clean glass container in the refrigerator for later use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat edema and conditions associated with fluid retention, including hypertension and congestive heart failure.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga capsule is a herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, anxiety, cholesterol, inflammation, pain, arthritis, diabetes, and edema. It is a widely used preparation in traditional medicine, often employed by healthcare professionals due to its efficacy. The common effect associated with ammi visnaga capsule is a reduction in blood pressure and inflammation, making it a popular choice for individuals suffering from these conditions. The capsule is typically made from the seeds and roots of the ammi visnaga plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. The process of making ammi visnaga capsule is moderately difficult, requiring careful extraction and concentration of the plant's active ingredients.
preparation_recipe: Dry and clean the seeds and roots of Ammi visnaga plant.
Grind the seeds and roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder of seeds with the powder of roots in a specific ratio.
Add a binding agent to the powder mixture to form a uniform paste.
Fill the paste into empty vegetable capsules to create the herbal capsules.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Essential oil made from this plant is used to treat cardiovascular conditions, reduce inflammation and lower blood pressure.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga essential oil is used to treat inflammation, gout, asthma, rheumatism, bronchitis, insect bites, eczema, anxiety, and fever. It is a commonly used preparation in traditional medicine, particularly in the Middle East and North Africa. One of the common effects of ammi visnaga essential oil is its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions. This preparation is made from the seeds, roots, and flowers of the Ammi visnaga plant, which requires careful harvesting and processing to produce the essential oil. Due to its complex composition and sensitive nature, making ammi visnaga essential oil is a challenging process that requires expertise and specialized equipment.
preparation_recipe: Harvest Ammi visnaga seeds, roots, and flowers at their peak potency.
Dry the seeds, roots, and flowers thoroughly in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Steam distill the dried seeds, roots, and flowers to extract their essential oils.
Collect and separate the resulting essential oil from the distillation process.
Store the extracted Ammi visnaga herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place to preserve its properties.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat gastrointestinal disorders such as dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Ammi visnaga suppository is a preparation used to treat constipation, haemorrhoids, fissures, pruritus, eczema and psoriasis. It is an uncommonly used treatment option for these conditions. Ammi visnaga suppository has a unique effect of promoting the healing of mucous membranes and reducing inflammation. The suppository is made from the roots and seeds of the Ammi visnaga plant, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Due to the complexity of the extraction process, the preparation of Ammi visnaga suppository is a challenging task that requires specialized knowledge and equipment.
preparation_recipe: Dry the Ammi visnaga roots and seeds in a warm place to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried Ammi visnaga roots and seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered roots and seeds with a binding agent such as beeswax or cocoa butter to create a uniform mixture.
Shape the mixture into small suppository forms using a suppository mold or your hands.
Store the Ammi visnaga herbal suppositories in an airtight container in a cool, dry place until use.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Ammi visnaga are exacerbate existing kidney problems, induce seizures in people with a history of epilepsy, aggravate cardiovascular conditions such as high blood pressure, prolong the bleeding time due to its anticoagulant properties, enhance the effects of certain medications like warfarin, trigger allergic reactions in sensitive individuals, amplify the risk of gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea and stomach cramps, worsen respiratory problems in people with asthma, increase the risk of liver damage, and interact negatively with other herbs like ginkgo biloba.

precautions.precaution_name: Consult a healthcare professional before use
precautions.precaution_list_description: Amni visnaga may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners, and excessive use can lead to complications such as bleeding.
Exceeding recommended dosages increases the risk of adverse effects.

precautions.precaution_name: Be aware of potential allergic reactions
precautions.precaution_list_description: Some individuals may experience allergic reactions to Ammi visnaga, including skin irritation and respiratory issues.

precautions.precaution_name: Monitor blood pressure and heart rate
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ammi visnaga can lower blood pressure and heart rate due to its coumarin content, which affects the cardiovascular system.

precautions.precaution_name: Avoid use during pregnancy and breastfeeding
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ammi visnaga has not been studied in pregnant or breastfeeding women, therefore its use is contraindicated.

precautions.precaution_name: Do not use with other hypertensive medications
precautions.precaution_list_description: Use with other hypertensive medications is contraindicated due to potential additive effect on blood pressure.

precautions.precaution_name: Be cautious with kidney and liver disease
precautions.precaution_list_description: Ammi visnaga may exacerbate kidney and liver disease due to its coumarin and umbelliferone content.
Monitor for symptoms such as increased heart rate and blood pressure in individuals consuming Ammi visnaga.

precautions.precaution_name: Use under medical supervision in children
precautions.precaution_list_description: Children should be monitored by a healthcare professional when using Ammi visnaga.

precautions.precaution_name: Store in a safe, inaccessible location
precautions.precaution_list_description: Protect from children and pets to prevent accidental ingestion.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Ammi visnaga has been found to have several positive effects, including its use as an antioxidant, which helps to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, and its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infections. The study also shows that Ammi visnaga has a powerful effect on facilitating the passage of kidney stones and relieving renal colic, providing relief for those suffering from these conditions. Additionally, its ability to relax smooth muscle in the coronary arteries can help to alleviate symptoms of coronary insufficiency, making it a valuable treatment option for these conditions.

According to a study published by "Planta medica", the most active extract of Ammi visnaga fruits, known as DAB 10, has been found to have a calcium antagonistic mode of action. This mode of action is primarily attributed to visnadin, a dihydropyranocoumarin, which was identified as the most active principle in the study. The presence of visnadin in Ammi visnaga suggests that it can be used to inhibit spasms and potentially provide relief in conditions related to calcium channel dysregulation.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Ammi visnaga are primarily attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which prevents the growth of various pathogens and inhibits their reproduction. This plant's antioxidant properties protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate swelling and pain by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, Ammi visnaga has analgesic properties, which help to relieve pain by inhibiting the activity of pain-producing neurons, and antispasmodic properties that calm muscle spasms and prevent cramping. Furthermore, its exectorant properties help to relieve coughs and congestion by loosening mucus and making it easier to expel. The plant's pain-relieving properties are also a result of the bioactive compounds present in it, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and coumarins, among other compounds.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Ammi visnaga are coumarins, specifically scopoletin which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, flavonoids including kaempferol and quercetin that exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, phenolic acids like ferulic acid which has anti-inflammatory effects, saponins that contribute to its anti-inflammatory properties by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, and furanocoumarins such as bergapten and psoralen which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The coumarins in Ammi visnaga have been shown to have a role in preventing the formation of blood clots and have been used as a natural anticoagulant. The antioxidant properties of these compounds are believed to contribute to the plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, the furanocoumarins in Ammi visnaga have been found to exhibit anti-cancer properties by inducing apoptosis in cancer cells. The combination of these bioactive compounds in Ammi visnaga may contribute to its traditional use in treating a variety of ailments including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Ammi visnaga for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments including fever and rheumatism due to their antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. Tinctures are often used in conjunction with a salve to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as the salve's topical application allows for targeted delivery of the plant's active compounds. A decoction, on the other hand, is a more potent preparation that can be used to treat more severe conditions such as epilepsy and meningeal inflammation, as it allows for the extraction of a higher concentration of bioactive compounds from the plant. Capsules are a less common preparation of Ammi visnaga, but are sometimes used as a convenient dosage form for the plant's antispasmodic and diuretic properties, particularly in cases of urinary tract infections. Additionally, the essential oil extracted from Ammi visnaga can be used to produce suppositories, which are inserted rectally to treat conditions such as hemorrhoids and anal fissures.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Ammi visnaga medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional before use to determine its safety and efficacy, as its dosages should be followed carefully to avoid adverse effects, including the risk of potential allergic reactions such as skin rashes and respiratory issues that may necessitate monitoring of blood pressure and heart rate, while also avoiding use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and not using it with other hypertensive medications, as this may exacerbate kidney and liver disease, and to watch for signs of overstimulation and use under medical supervision in children, and store it in a safe, inaccessible location.
